Case title: The AI Scientist / AI Scientist-v2 as end-to-end automation boundary cases
Short description: Use fully automated scientific-discovery systems as boundary cases to see which role claims are social-science-specific and which generalize across autonomous research.
Empirical unit of analysis: Autonomous papers, review scores, and workflow traces
Candidate roles tested: Protocol and provenance architect; accountability and labor allocator; relevance adjudicator
Key typology question: Which parts of the typology are general to autonomous research, and which are specific to social science?
Observable artifacts / traces: Workflow traces, automated reviews, paper artifacts, acceptance outcomes
Key outcomes observed: Autonomy claims, review outcomes, human intervention points
What would weaken / falsify the role cla: Autonomous systems generalize cleanly to social-science workflows without role-specific adjustments
Main confounds / risks of misreading: Most evidence comes from AI/ML research rather than social science
Why this case has high leverage: Useful only as a boundary case; it should not be mistaken for direct evidence about social science.
collector_notes: Generated from workbook and workbook-linked sources.
